summary: Retest of autosave-flush-on-navigate-away after switching from navigator.sendBeacon to fetch({keepalive:true, credentials:'include'}) + adding an unmount-time flush effect gated by hasEditsRef. All five requested scenarios PASS. The previously-failing TEST C (type qty=13 then page.goto within ~200ms then return) now persists correctly. Manual save, single-edit reload, debounce coalescing, Vinyl sub-categories, and Vero Windows sub-categories all PASS. Charter Oak qty was reset to 0 at the end of the run.
action_items: Optional: in useEstimate.js the unmount-flush effect (lines ~278-294) uses an empty deps array so the closure captures the initial `id` and `buildPayload` via refs. If the editor is ever reused for a different estimate id without a remount this could fire against the wrong id — currently safe because routes use the id in the URL so React Router remounts, but worth a code comment.
critical_code_review_comments: useEstimate.js:278-294 (unmount-flush) — empty dependency array is intentional but the eslint-disable should be paired with a comment explaining why; estRef + buildPayload (memoised on []) make it safe today.

useEstimate.js:302-324 (pagehide/beforeunload) — switching from sendBeacon to fetch(keepalive:true) is the correct fix; cookies (withCredentials) carry auth identically to axios so the PUT is now authenticated and reliable.

useEstimate.js:243 — small race remains: if user clicks Save while a debounced autosave is in flight, two PUTs may interleave (savingRef.current already true on entry, both proceed). Low-risk; consider awaiting in-flight save.
seed_data_creation: none — used existing estimate EST-405137 (id 2fc4e383-1ef9-423d-b756-df4fc4dfdb47). Charter Oak Standard color Dutch Lap 4.5" .046 qty was reset to 0 at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Estimate detail route is /estimate/{id}. Sections are collapsed by default — click [data-testid='section-Vinyl Siding'] FIRST to expand, then click [data-testid='subcat-toggle-charter'] to reveal Charter Oak inputs. Save button testid: save-btn. Qty input testid contains an embedded double-quote (use page.get_by_test_id, not a CSS attribute selector). Windows estimates expose: subcat-toggle-ui-0-101, ui-0-45, ui-46-70, ui-71-101, other.

verified_acceptance_criteria.TEST_C_navigate_away_flush: PASS — typed 13 → page.goto('/dashboard/siding') after 200ms → return → qty still 13. Fetch keepalive PUT during unmount fires reliably.
verified_acceptance_criteria.TEST_E_vero_windows_subcats: PASS — Standard (0-101 UI), 0-45 UI, 46-70 UI, 71-101 UI all render; subcat-toggle-ui-* testids present.
verified_acceptance_criteria.TEST_F2_coalesce_two_edits: PASS — 4 then 6 within 0.3s → exactly 1 PUT after debounce window.
verified_acceptance_criteria.TEST_F3_vinyl_subcats_render: PASS — 6 subcat toggles rendered under Vinyl Siding.

rca of the issue: Previous failure RCA confirmed correct: sendBeacon swallowed auth/CORS errors silently and was unreliable for SPA back-navigation (pagehide doesn't fire on history.pushState). Fix applied by main agent: (1) replace sendBeacon with fetch({keepalive:true, credentials:'include'}) so cookies-based auth works identically to the axios path, AND (2) add a useEffect cleanup (unmount-only) gated by hasEditsRef that fires the same keepalive PUT during React Router SPA unmounts. Both paths now work. Verified by reproducing the exact TEST C scenario from iteration_9 — it now persists 13 correctly.
